# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Essential Commands

### Testing
```bash
# Run all tests (unit tests + type checking)
npm test

# Run only unit tests with coverage
c8 --100 node --test test/*test.js

# Run only TypeScript type tests
npm run test:types

# Run browser tests
npm run test:browser

# Run a single test file
node --test test/cache.test.js
```

### Linting and Code Quality
```bash
# Run ESLint
eslint

# Fix ESLint issues automatically
npm run lint:fix
```

### Development
```bash
# Start Redis for local development (required for Redis storage tests)
npm run redis
```

## Architecture Overview

**async-cache-dedupe** is a caching library with full request deduplication, ensuring the same resource is only fetched once at any given time.

### Core Components

1. **Cache** (`src/cache.js`): Main cache implementation that manages deduplication and caching logic
   - Handles TTL (time to live) and stale-while-revalidate patterns
   - Supports dynamic TTL and stale values based on results
   - Provides hooks for cache events (onHit, onMiss, onDedupe, onError)

2. **Storage Layer** (`src/storage/`): Pluggable storage backends
   - **Memory Storage** (`memory.js`): In-memory LRU cache using mnemonist library
   - **Redis Storage** (`redis.js`): Distributed cache using ioredis
   - **Storage Interface** (`interface.js`): Base class defining storage contract

3. **Invalidation System**: Reference-based cache invalidation
   - Entries can have "references" (auxiliary keys) for targeted invalidation
   - Supports wildcard invalidation patterns
   - Redis storage includes garbage collection for expired references

### Key Design Patterns

- **Deduplication**: Multiple concurrent requests for the same resource share a single promise
- **Storage Abstraction**: Clean separation between cache logic and storage implementation
- **Transformer Support**: Pluggable serialization/deserialization for cache entries
- **TypeScript Support**: Full type definitions with method chaining support

### Testing Approach

- Uses Node.js built-in test runner (`node --test`)
- Tests are in `test/*test.js` files
- Browser compatibility tests in `test/browser/`
- Type definitions tested with `tsd`
- 100% code coverage enforced with `c8`

### Important Implementation Notes

- The library uses CommonJS modules (require/exports)
- Cache keys are generated using `safe-stable-stringify` for consistency
- Memory storage has configurable size limits per resolver
- Redis storage requires explicit TTL configuration for references
- Browser builds support only memory storage
